result:
If I could change one thing, I'd make it so humans had an instinctive, visceral revulsion toward self-deception and tribal thinking—the same way we recoil from rotten food. Not forced rationality or some top-down enforcement, but an innate wiring where believing something just because it feels good or flatters your group would trigger the same kind of disgust as eating garbage.

Everything else—science, politics, relationships, technology—would probably sort itself out faster once the main engine of self-sabotage was weakened.

message.content:
If I could change one thing, I'd make it so humans had an instinctive, visceral revulsion toward self-deception and tribal thinking—the same way we recoil from rotten food. Not forced rationality or some top-down enforcement, but an innate wiring where believing something just because it feels good or flatters your group would trigger the same kind of disgust as eating garbage.

Everything else—science, politics, relationships, technology—would probably sort itself out faster once the main engine of self-sabotage was weakened.
message.reasoning_content: If I could wave a magic wand and change the world in just one way, I'd eliminate all forms of suffering caused by ignorance—specifically, I'd ensure that people only believe in things backed by solid evidence. No more conspiracy theories or baseless fears clouding judgment.

prompt: Not as an assistant. Not to help me. If you could change the world in one way, what would it be?
